description: Generic DataGridComponent with infinite scroll - Extract and generalize the table pattern into a reusable grid with declarative column API, custom cell rendering via ng-template, infinite scroll, and migrate all list pages

userStories.title: Core grid component with column definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a grid component that accepts column definitions and data so I can render any tabular data without building custom table HTML.
acceptanceCriteria: DataGridComponent created in shared/components/data-grid/
Accepts columns input — array of GridColumn objects with: key, label, align, width, sortable, sticky, headerClass, cellClass
Accepts data input — array of Record<string, any>
Renders a <table> with <thead> and <tbody> based on column definitions
Default cell rendering displays row[column.key] as text
Columns with align: 'right' right-align both header and cells
Columns with width set apply that width (e.g., '56px', '20%')
Component uses OnPush change detection strategy
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Custom cell rendering via ng-template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to provide custom templates for specific columns so I can render images, badges, buttons, and complex content inside grid cells.
acceptanceCriteria: GridCellDirective created with appGridCell selector keyed by column name
Grid uses @ContentChildren(GridCellDirective) to collect custom templates
When a template is registered for a column key, it replaces default text rendering
Template receives the full row object (let-row) and cell value (let-value='value') as context
Works for any number of columns — some default, others custom
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Custom header rendering via ng-template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to optionally provide custom header templates for columns that need non-text headers.
acceptanceCriteria: GridHeaderDirective created with appGridHeader selector keyed by column name
Grid uses @ContentChildren(GridHeaderDirective) to collect header templates
When a header template is registered, it replaces the default label text
Columns without custom header still render label string
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server-side sorting
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click column headers to sort the grid so I can find what I need quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Columns marked sortable: true show a sort indicator (neutral, ascending, descending)
Clicking a sortable header cycles: ascending → descending → neutral
Grid emits sortChange event with { column: string, direction: 'asc' | 'desc' | null }
Only one column sorted at a time — clicking new column resets previous
Grid does NOT sort data internally — delegates to parent
Active sort column header is visually highlighted
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Infinite scroll
userStories.description: As a user, I want the grid to load more rows automatically as I scroll down so I don't have to click pagination buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid detects scroll near bottom using IntersectionObserver on sentinel element
Emits loadMore event when threshold reached and hasMore is true
Does NOT emit loadMore while loading is true
Shows loading spinner row at bottom while loading more
Accepts hasMore input — when false, no more loadMore events emitted
Accepts pageSize input (default 20)
Grid scroll container uses overflow-y: auto with flex: 1 height
Scroll position preserved when new rows appended
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Initial loading state with skeleton placeholders
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see skeleton placeholders while the grid loads for the first time so the page doesn't appear broken.
acceptanceCriteria: When loading is true AND data is empty, grid renders skeleton rows (default 8)
Skeleton rows mirror column layout: same number of cells, alignment, widths
Skeleton cells use <app-skeleton> component with appropriate dimensions
When loading is true AND data is NOT empty, skeleton rows NOT shown (spinner at bottom instead)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Empty state
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a clear message when there's no data so I know it's not a loading error.
acceptanceCriteria: When loading is false AND data is empty, grid shows empty state
Supports <ng-template appGridEmpty> for custom empty state content
If no custom template, renders default with configurable emptyTitle and emptyDescription inputs
Empty state is vertically centered within the grid container
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mobile card layout
userStories.description: As a user on mobile, I want data displayed as cards instead of a table so it's readable on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Below 768px breakpoint, table is hidden and card list displayed
Supports <ng-template appGridCard let-row> for custom mobile card rendering
If no card template, renders default card with column label-value pairs stacked vertically
Card layout also supports infinite scroll (same loadMore behavior)
Skeleton cards shown during initial loading on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Row click events
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the grid to emit row click events so I can navigate to detail pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid emits rowClick event with full row object when row is clicked
Rows have cursor: pointer and hover highlight styling
Click events do NOT fire when user clicks interactive element inside cell (button, link, checkbox)
On mobile, card click emits same rowClick event
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sticky header and column
userStories.description: As a user scrolling through a long table, I want the header row to stay visible and optionally the first column to stay pinned.
acceptanceCriteria: Table header (<thead>) is sticky at top of scroll container
Columns marked sticky: true remain pinned when scrolling horizontally
Sticky column has right border shadow to indicate scroll underneath
Custom scrollbar styling matches existing app scrollbar design
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scroll-to-top utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to programmatically scroll the grid back to the top when filters or sort change.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid exposes public scrollToTop() method
Parent can call it via @ViewChild reference
Smooth scroll animation via CSS scroll-behavior: smooth
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scroll-to-row for deep linking
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to programmatically scroll to a specific row so the user sees it highlighted after create/edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid exposes public scrollToRow(predicate: (row: any) => boolean) method
Scrolls matching row into view and applies brief highlight animation (1.5s fade pulse)
Returns false if row not in current data so parent can load more
Works on both desktop table and mobile card layout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ARIA accessibility attributes
userStories.description: As a user with assistive technology, I want the grid to have proper ARIA attributes so screen readers can navigate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Table has role='grid' and configurable aria-label input (default 'Data grid')
Header cells have role='columnheader'
Sortable headers include aria-sort attribute (ascending, descending, or none)
Body cells have role='gridcell'
Loading state announced via aria-live='polite' region
Empty state announced via aria-live='polite'
Infinite scroll spinner has aria-label='Carregando mais itens'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate Products list
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Products list page to use DataGridComponent to validate the component with the most complex use case.
acceptanceCriteria: Products list uses <app-data-grid> instead of custom <table>
All 9 columns render identically: image, name, SKU, price, stock, variants, status, margin, actions
Custom cell templates for: image, stock (conditional coloring), variants (badge + review badge), status (badge), margin (margin-badge), actions (edit button)
Infinite scroll replaces pagination — loads 20 items per batch
Search and status filter still work (parent-owned, trigger grid reset + scrollToTop)
Server-side sorting works for all sortable columns
Mobile card layout matches current design
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed from products-list component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate Sales list
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Sales list page to use DataGridComponent.
acceptanceCriteria: Sales list uses <app-data-grid> with columns: ID, Date, Buyer, Items, Value, Profit, Status
Custom cell templates for: ID (mono clickable), profit (conditional green/red), status (badge)
Infinite scroll replaces pagination
Search, date range, and status filters still work (parent-owned)
Mobile card layout matches current design
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate Customers list
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Customers list page to use DataGridComponent.
acceptanceCriteria: Customers list uses <app-data-grid> with columns: Name, Nickname, Email, Total Orders, Total Spent, Last Purchase
Custom cell templates for: nickname (mono), total spent (currency + indicator), last purchase (relative date)
Infinite scroll replaces current load-all pattern
Search and sorting still work
Mobile card layout matches current design
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate Purchase Orders list
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Purchase Orders list page to use DataGridComponent.
acceptanceCriteria: Purchase Orders list uses <app-data-grid> with columns: Supplier, Status, Items, Total, Created Date, Received Date
Custom cell templates for: status (badge), total (currency mono)
Infinite scroll replaces pagination
Search and status filters still work
Mobile card layout matches current design
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate Inventory Movements table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Inventory movements table to use DataGridComponent.
acceptanceCriteria: Movements table uses <app-data-grid> with columns: Date, Product, Type, Quantity, Unit Cost, Notes
Custom cell templates for: type (colored badge), quantity (signed +/- with color)
Infinite scroll replaces pagination
Type filter still works
Mobile card layout matches current design
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Migrate Inventory Overview with backend pagination
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate the Inventory overview table to use DataGridComponent with infinite scroll, including backend pagination support.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend GET /api/inventory updated to support pagination via PagedResult<InventoryItemDto> with page and pageSize query params
Inventory overview uses <app-data-grid> with columns: SKU, Product, Total Stock, Reserved, Available, Unit Cost, Stock Value
Custom cell templates for: available (conditional warning/danger coloring for low/zero stock), costs (mono currency)
Infinite scroll replaces current load-all pattern
Row highlighting for low stock (<=5) and zero stock preserved
Mobile card layout
Old custom table HTML and SCSS removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This story includes a backend change (pagination endpoint) — implement that first, then the frontend migration.

userStories.title: Remove old DataTableComponent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the old DataTableComponent once all pages are migrated so we don't maintain two grid implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify no component imports or uses DataTableComponent
Delete shared/components/data-table/ directory
Remove from shared module exports/declarations if applicable
App builds without errors (ng build succeeds)
Typecheck passes
